segments.text: After this, Jesus spent his time going from place to place in Galilee. He did not want to do so in Judea because the Jews were out to kill him.
But as it was almost time for the Jewish festival of the Tabernacles,
his brothers told him, {
You ought to leave and go to Judea so your followers will be able to see what miracles you can do.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No one who wants to be famous keeps what they do hidden. If you can do such miracles, then show yourself to the world!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For even his own brothers really didn't believe in him.
Jesus told them, {
This is not my time to go, not yet; but you can go whenever you want, for any time's the right time for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The world has no reason to hate you, but it does hate me, because I make it clear that its ways are evil.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You go on to the festival. I'm not going to this festival because this is not the right time for me, not yet.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After saying this he stayed behind in Galilee.
After his brothers left to go to the festival, Jesus also went, but not openly—he stayed out of sight.
Now at the festival the Jewish leaders were searching for him and kept on asking, {
Where is he?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many people in the crowds were complaining about him. Some said, {
He's a good man,
segments.speaker: Some

segments.text: while others argued,
No! He deceives people.
segments.speaker: Some

segments.text: But no one dared to speak openly about him because they were afraid of what the Jewish leaders would do to them.
When the festival was halfway through, Jesus went to the Temple and began to teach.
The Jewish leaders were very surprised, and asked, {
How does this man have so much learning[^48] when he hasn't been educated?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus answered, {
My teaching is not from me but from the one who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Anyone who chooses to follow what God wants will know if my teaching comes from God or if I'm only speaking for myself.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who speak for themselves want to glorify themselves, but someone who glorifies the one who sent him is truthful and not deceitful.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Moses gave you the law, didn't he? Yet none of you keeps the law! Why are you trying to kill me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You're demon-possessed!
segments.speaker: The crowd

segments.text: the crowd replied.
No one's trying to kill you!
segments.speaker: The crowd

segments.text: I did one miracle[^49] and you're all shocked by it,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
However, because Moses told you to circumcise—not that it really came from Moses, but from your forefathers before him—you perform circumcision on the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you circumcise on the Sabbath to make sure that the law of Moses isn't broken, why are you angry with me for healing someone on the Sabbath?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't judge by appearances; decide what is right!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then some of those from Jerusalem began wondering, {
Isn't this the one they're trying to kill?
segments.speaker: some of those from Jerusalem

segments.text: But see how openly he's speaking, and they're saying nothing to him. Do you think the authorities believe he's the Messiah?
segments.speaker: some of those from Jerusalem

segments.text: But that's not possible because we know where he comes from. When the Messiah comes, nobody will know where he's from.
segments.speaker: some of those from Jerusalem

segments.text: While he was teaching in the Temple, Jesus called out in a loud voice, {
So you think you know me and where I'm from? However, I did not come for my own sake. The one who sent me is true. You don't know him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but I know him, for I come from him, and he sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they tried to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him because his time had not yet come.
However, many of the crowd did put their trust in him. {
When the Messiah appears, will he do more miraculous signs than this man has done?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said.
When the Pharisees heard the crowd whispering this about him, they and the chief priests sent guards to arrest Jesus.
Then Jesus told the people, {
I'll be with you just a little longer, but then I'll return to the one who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You'll search for me but you won't find me; and you can't come where I'm going.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Jews said to each other, {
Where could he be going that we couldn't find him? Is he planning to go to those scattered among the foreigners, [^50] and teach the foreigners?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: What does he mean by saying, ‘You'll search for me but you won't find me; and you can't come where I'm going’?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted out in a loud voice, {
If you're thirsty, come to me and drink.
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: If you trust in me, you will have streams of life-giving water flowing out from within you, as Scripture says.
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: He was referring to the Spirit that those who trusted in him would later receive. The Spirit hadn't been given yet because Jesus hadn't yet been glorified.
When they heard these words, some of the people said, {
This man is definitely the Prophet!
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Others said, {
He is the Messiah!
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: Still others said,
How can the Messiah come from Galilee?
segments.speaker: Still others

segments.text: Doesn't Scripture say that the Messiah comes from David's lineage, and from David's home town of Bethlehem?
segments.speaker: Still others

segments.text: So the crowd had a strong difference of opinion about him.
Some wanted to arrest him, but nobody laid a hand on him.
Then the guards returned to the chief priests and the Pharisees who asked them, {
Why didn't you bring him in?
segments.speaker: Still others

segments.text: Nobody ever spoke like this man does,
segments.speaker: The guards

segments.text: the guards replied.
Have you been fooled too?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: the Pharisees asked them.
Has a single one of the rulers or Pharisees believed in him? No!
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: But this crowd knows nothing about teachings of the law—they're damned anyway!
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: Nicodemus, who had previously gone to meet Jesus, was one of them and asked them,
Does our law condemn a man without a hearing and without finding out what he actually did?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: So you're a Galilean as well, are you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied.
Check the Scriptures and you'll discover that no prophet comes from Galilee!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then they all went home, [^54]
